evals.prompt: Set up a Data Access Layer in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Create lib/dal.ts with import 'server-only' at top; export async function getUser(id: string): Promise<UserDTO> { const session = await verifySession(); const user = await db.user.findUnique({where:{id}}); return toDTO(user); }

evals.prompt: Use server-only package to protect DAL files in Next.js
evals.expected_output: import 'server-only' at top of dal.ts and lib/crypto.ts; causes build error if any Client Component imports the file; prevents DB credentials and server logic from reaching client bundle

evals.prompt: Return DTOs from server functions in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Map raw DB result: function toDTO(user: User): UserDTO { return { id: user.id, name: user.name }; }; never return Prisma model instance; prevents accidental serialization of relations or sensitive fields

evals.prompt: Centralize auth checks in a DAL function
evals.expected_output: Auth check inside DAL: const session = await verifySession(); if (!session.userId) redirect('/login'); never rely on caller to check auth; each DAL function is self-contained and secure

evals.prompt: Avoid fetching own API routes from Server Components
evals.expected_output: Never fetch('http://localhost:3000/api/users') from RSC; call DAL function directly: const users = await getUsers(); removes unnecessary HTTP round trip and serialization overhead

evals.prompt: Build a BFF pattern with Next.js and NestJS
evals.expected_output: Pattern A (BFF): DAL fetches from NestJS: const res = await fetch(process.env.API_URL + '/users', { headers: { Authorization } }); Pattern B (Direct): DAL calls Prisma directly; both use import 'server-only'

should_not_trigger: Repository pattern in Laravel with Eloquent
Data access in Spring Boot with JPA Repository
